description: Make Stratum's per-step `agent:` field interpolatable so a flow can select its executor at runtime. Today `Step.agent` is a literal string emitted verbatim by the executor (spec.py:102); `inputs` are rendered (`$.input.*` / `$.steps.*.output.*`) but the agent field is not. Resolve the agent field through the same resolver as inputs before `make_agent_connector`, so `agent: "$.input.executor"` or `agent: "$.steps.route.output.agent"` (a router/triage step emitting the agent for a later step) works. Stays in the data plane: the resolved value comes from recorded flow state, so audit/resume/result-cache replay identically — no control-plane hook, no non-deterministic external actor in the executor loop. Backward-compatible: literal agent strings (no `$.`) behave exactly as today; a resolved value must validate to a known agent type or the step errors before dispatch. Enables spec-controlled dynamic executor selection (e.g. claude vs codex per task) — the data-plane alternative to a mid-flow orchestrator override, and a natural enabler for COMP-CODEX-IMPL. Owner: Stratum (stratum-mcp executor/IR); compose consumes transparently since build.js already honors response.agent.

completions.notes: Canon backfill. Feature is stratum-owned (code lives in the stratum repo) but compose-tracked. Implemented + pushed in stratum commit 4651933 (ancestor of stratum origin/main @ 9099e2a); shipped tests in test_agent_interp.py (stratum suite green); already depended on by the COMPLETE COMP-CODEX-IMPL. feature.json was left PLANNED because record_completion/set_feature_status could not run: capabilities.guard's verifyCompletionEvidence does server-read git verification against the COMPOSE repo, where the stratum commit is invisible (cross-repo ownership gap). Status set by direct file edit with evidence independently verified, in lieu of the unavailable guard path. Cross-repo evidence gap to be filed as a compose follow-up.
